en: Better is a dry morsel with quiet than a house full of feasting with strife.
zh: 平靜相安地吃一塊乾餅， 勝過筵席滿屋，吵鬧相爭。

en: A servant who deals wisely will rule over a son who acts shamefully and will share the inheritance as one of the brothers.
zh: 明慧的僕人，必管轄主人貽羞的兒子； 又可以在眾兒子中同分產業。

en: The crucible is for silver, and the furnace is for gold, and the LORD tests hearts.
zh: 用鍋煉銀，用爐煉金； 唯有耶和華鍛煉人心。

en: An evildoer listens to wicked lips, and a liar gives ear to a mischievous tongue.
zh: 作惡的人留心聽邪惡的話； 說謊的人側耳聽攻擊人的話。

en: Whoever mocks the poor insults his Maker; he who is glad at calamity will not go unpunished.
zh: 嘲笑窮人的就是辱罵造他的主； 幸災樂禍的必難免受懲罰。

en: Grandchildren are the crown of the aged, and the glory of children is their fathers.
zh: 兒孫是老人的冠冕， 父親是兒女的榮耀。

en: Fine speech is not becoming to a fool; still less is false speech to a prince.
zh: 愚頑人說佳美的話是不相稱的， 何況尊貴的人說虛謊的話呢！

en: A bribe is like a magic stone in the eyes of the one who gives it; wherever he turns he prospers.
zh: 在餽送的人看來，賄賂有如靈符 ； 無論他到哪裡，都必順利。

en: Whoever covers an offense seeks love, but he who repeats a matter separates close friends.
zh: 遮掩別人過犯的，得到人的喜愛； 屢次提起別人過錯的，離間親密的朋友。

en: A rebuke goes deeper into a man of understanding than a hundred blows into a fool.
zh: 對聰明人說一句責備的話， 勝過責打愚昧人一百下。

en: An evil man seeks only rebellion, and a cruel messenger will be sent against him.
zh: 悖逆的人只求惡事， 必有殘忍的使者奉派去對付他。

en: Let a man meet a she-bear robbed of her cubs rather than a fool in his folly.
zh: 寧願遇見失掉幼子的母熊， 也不願遇見正在行愚妄事的愚昧人。

en: If anyone returns evil for good, evil will not depart from his house.
zh: 以惡報善的， 災禍必不離開他的家。

en: The beginning of strife is like letting out water, so quit before the quarrel breaks out.
zh: 紛爭的開始，如同決堤的水； 所以在爭執發生以前，就要制止。

en: He who justifies the wicked and he who condemns the righteous are both alike an abomination to the LORD.
zh: 宣判惡人為義，裁定義人有罪， 二者都是耶和華所厭惡的。

en: Why should a fool have money in his hand to buy wisdom when he has no sense?
zh: 愚昧人既是無知， 為甚麼手裡拿著價銀要買智慧呢？

en: A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.
zh: 朋友常顯愛心， 兄弟為患難而生。

en: One who lacks sense gives a pledge and puts up security in the presence of his neighbor.
zh: 為鄰舍擊掌作保證人的， 是無知的人。

en: Whoever loves transgression loves strife; he who makes his door high seeks destruction.
zh: 喜愛爭競的就是喜愛過犯； 把家門建高的自取滅亡。

en: A man of crooked heart does not discover good, and one with a dishonest tongue falls into calamity.
zh: 心存欺詐的得不著益處； 舌頭搬弄是非的必陷在禍患中。

en: He who sires a fool gets himself sorrow, and the father of a fool has no joy.
zh: 生下愚昧的兒子，使父親憂愁； 愚頑人的父親毫無喜樂。

en: A joyful heart is good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.
zh: 心裡喜樂就是良藥； 心靈憂鬱使骨頭枯乾。

en: The wicked accepts a bribe in secret to pervert the ways of justice.
zh: 惡人暗中 接受賄賂， 為要歪曲公正。

en: The discerning sets his face toward wisdom, but the eyes of a fool are on the ends of the earth.
zh: 聰明人面前有智慧， 愚昧人卻眼望地極。

en: A foolish son is a grief to his father and bitterness to her who bore him.
zh: 愚昧的兒子使父親愁煩， 使母親痛苦。

en: To impose a fine on a righteous man is not good, nor to strike the noble for their uprightness.
zh: 懲罰義人，已是不當； 擊打正直的官長，更是不妥。

en: Whoever restrains his words has knowledge, and he who has a cool spirit is a man of understanding.
zh: 有知識的約束自己的言語； 聰明人心平氣和。

en: Even a fool who keeps silent is considered wise; when he closes his lips, he is deemed intelligent.
zh: 愚妄人默不作聲，也算是智慧； 閉口不言，也算是聰明。
